commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can restore actionable fallback text and update tests; maintainer approval-safety review remains required after that repair and rebase.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive because it summarizes protected plugin command approvals, and one opt-in text path currently leaves the manual approval fallback non-actionable.; Review finding: [P1] Keep plugin approval fallbacks act... (sha=a127fcaea76ff73b50367dd98f3e69b2c2a2d75a)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an opt-in `approvals.plugin.language` config for plain-language plugin approval prompts, wires it through shared rendering plus Telegram/Matrix delivery, updates SDK/config/docs baselines, and adds focused approval-renderer tests.

PR surface: Source +3711, Tests +2783, Docs +10, Generated 0. Total +6504 across 41 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows simple-technical mode omits the actual-id fallback and the PR tests assert the placeholder form. The PR itself is a new feature, so there is no existing-bug reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can restore actionable fallback text and update tests; maintainer approval-safety review remains required after that repair and rebase.

target.title: feat(approvals): add plain-language plugin approvals
target.body:
## Mission

Plugin approvals are a security boundary, but they are often delivered in chat where the old fallback text read like internal debug output. A normal approval could show a raw title, full command text, proposed exec policy, session key, tool/plugin IDs, expiry, and `/approve <id>` syntax all at once.

That raw format is useful for maintainers, but it is not easy for a Telegram/Matrix user to scan quickly. The goal of this PR is to make opt-in plugin approvals understandable at a glance while preserving the legacy raw behavior by default.

In short: keep the original technical approval prompt as the default, and add a plain-language approval mode for users who explicitly choose it.

## What This Adds

This PR adds `approvals.plugin.language` with three modes:

- `original`: existing raw technical plugin approval text. This remains the default.
- `simple`: a short plain-language prompt with `Action`, `It will`, `Risk`, and available choices.
- `simple-technical`: the plain-language prompt plus the raw technical fields below it.

Example config:

```json
{
  "approvals": {
    "plugin": {
      "language": "simple"
    }
  }
}
```

The new renderer keeps interactive approval metadata intact so native Telegram/Matrix approval buttons can still be rendered. Text-only channels still get an actionable `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once|allow-always|deny` fallback.

## Implementation

- Added the typed `PluginApprovalLanguage` config surface and validation.
- Wired the configured language through shared plugin approval rendering.
- Forwarded the language option through fallback delivery and native Telegram/Matrix approval payloads.
- Added plain-English approval output with risk text and decision-aware fallback instructions.
- Added `Command preview` output for commands that are high risk or cannot be summarized completely.
- Redacted token/password/API-key/bearer/basic-auth/cookie/userinfo forms from previews before they can be posted into chat.
- Updated generated config and plugin SDK API baselines.
- Added focused renderer and forwarder tests.

## Safety Model

The important rule is: if `simple` mode hides raw technical details, the summary must either explain the meaningful action or fail closed.

Fail closed means the approval becomes higher risk and shows a redacted command preview instead of pretending the command is harmless.

The renderer now handles and tests common Codex app-server command shapes:

- shell wrappers: `bash -lc`, `sh -c`, `zsh -c`, `env`, `timeout`, `set -a`, `source`;
- shell control: `&&`, `||`, `;`, background `&`, conditionals, loops, and attached redirections like `cat notes.txt>out.txt`;
- pipelines: known stages are summarized stage-by-stage; unknown stages become high risk with a preview;
- file reads/writes/deletes: `cat`, `sed`, `find`, `rm`, `mkdir`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, redirection writes;
- network transfers: `curl` and `wget` URL targets, upload operands, output operands, stdin upload via redirection, config/header/cookie/auth/netrc forms;
- credentials: bearer headers, OAuth bearer flags, basic auth, proxy auth, cookie headers, netrc credential sources, URL userinfo;
- dev/ops commands: `git`, `docker`, service managers, package managers, interpreters, logs/process inspection;
- remote commands: `ssh`, `scp`, SSH remote commands, and SSH local-execution options such as `ProxyCommand` and `LocalCommand`.

## Review And Hardening Trail

This PR went through a long approval-safety review loop because turning verbose technical prompts into plain language makes the summarizer security-sensitive.

Issues fixed during review:

- Preserved actionable text fallback instructions for channels without buttons.
- Made pipelines visible before raw command text can be hidden.
- Made command target extraction command-aware so boolean flags like `cat -n .env` do not hide sensitive targets.
- Surfaced curl/wget upload and output operands, including `.env` style local-file transfers.
- Handled stdin uploads such as `curl --data-binary @- ... < .env`.
- Redacted basic-auth, OAuth bearer, cookie, header, and URL credential forms from command previews.
- Failed closed on curl config files and curl header-file inputs.
- Surfaced wget credential/header/cookie options.
- Showed targets for redirected formatter writes like `printf ... > .env`.
- Handled background shell separators such as `sleep 1 & rm -rf /tmp/x`.
- Parsed Git global options such as `git -C repo reset --hard` before classifying subcommands.
- Treated `find` output-file predicates like `-fprint`, `-fprint0`, `-fls`, and `-fprintf` as writes.
- Detected attached shell redirections like `cat notes.txt>out.txt`.
- Failed closed on SSH local-execution options such as `ssh -o ProxyCommand=...` and `ssh -o LocalCommand=...`.
- Refreshed against latest `upstream/main` repeatedly and regenerated the config baseline after conflicts.
- Stabilized cron test mock state exposed by the refreshed CI shard.

The latest ClawSweeper review no longer reports a concrete repair item. It now asks for human maintainer review of the opt-in approval-safety boundary and branch scope.

## Current Status

- Latest head at time of this update: `91d6303a0e`.
- GitHub reports the PR mergeable and clean.
- CI/checks are green on the latest head.
- `Real behavior proof` is successful.
- ClawSweeper durable review status: needs maintainer review before merge, not an automated repair.
- Remaining decision: maintainers need to accept the opt-in plain-language approval UX/security boundary, or ask for scope splitting if they want the unrelated refreshed-base/test-stability churn separated.

## Verification

Local verification after the latest SSH hardening and main refresh:

- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/plugin-approval-forwarder.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`

Earlier focused verification also covered config schema/help quality, plugin SDK API baseline, gateway/channel state, and live renderer output.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Chat-delivered plugin approvals can now render as a short plain-English approval card instead of a raw technical dump when `approvals.plugin.language` is set to `simple`. The old raw format remains the default through `original`.
- Real environment tested: Hadi's live OpenClaw source checkout on Ubuntu 24.04 (`/home/hadi/src/openclaw`), the PR worktree, and Telegram channel status in polling mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Rendered real approval messages through `buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage(..., { language: "simple" })`.
  - Ran focused approval renderer and forwarder tests.
  - Ran type, lint, config docs, and baseline checks.
  - Cherry-picked the feature into the live source checkout during testing, ran `pnpm build`, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, and checked gateway/Telegram channel state.
- Evidence after fix: terminal renderer output showed the intended card shape:

```text
🛡️ Approval needed
Action
Create/check workspace files or folders

It will
- create folder(s): memory
- check whether a condition or file exists: memory/context.md

Risk: Low
Only creates or checks workspace files. No network, service, or delete step detected.

Choose below.
If buttons are unavailable, reply: /approve plugin:example allow-once|allow-always|deny
```

Pipeline and unknown-stage proof:

```text
--- pipeline curl ---
🛡️ Approval needed
Action
Use the network or download data

It will
- read file contents: notes.txt
- make a network request or download data: https://example.test/upload

Risk: Medium
Network commands can send or fetch data outside this machine.

Choose below.
If buttons are unavailable, reply: /approve plugin:pipeline allow-once|allow-always|deny

--- unknown pipeline ---
🛡️ Approval needed
Action
Run a terminal command

It will
- read file contents: notes.txt
- run custom-uploader

Command preview
cat notes.txt | custom-uploader --send

Risk: High
This command is part of a pipeline I cannot fully summarize, so review it before approving.

Choose below.
If buttons are unavailable, reply: /approve plugin:unknown allow-once|allow-always|deny
```

Live channel state checked during proof:

```text
Telegram default (Telegram): enabled, configured, running, connected, mode:polling, token:tokenFile
```

Observed result after fix:

- `simple` mode no longer includes the raw technical details block.
- Escaped shell separators such as `&&` are decoded before parsing.
- Shell conditionals no longer surface as fake `run if` / `run then` actions.
- Non-shell pipeline stages are visible or fail closed with a high-risk preview.
- Sensitive command previews are redacted before being shown in chat.
- SSH local-execution options now fail closed with a high-risk preview.

- What was not tested:

- The Mantis Telegram Desktop GIF run skipped instead of producing before/after GIFs. This PR still has terminal renderer proof, live gateway/channel state proof, earlier Telegram screenshot proof, and a successful `Real behavior proof` check.

commands.author: RomneyDa

target.title: feat: GitHub App skill sync behind internal UI gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a GitHub App install/setup flow for publishers with HMAC-signed setup state, single-use state consumption, webhook signature verification, and installation webhook claims for org-account handshakes.
- Add repository links, source-link mappings, sync jobs, webhook delivery tracking, and source-managed publish enforcement so only linked skills sync while manual org skills remain untouched.
- Add repository sync from pinned GitHub archive commits, push-job coalescing/serialization, repository removal/rename handling, and source provenance checks.
- Gate the GitHub Sync UI for internal rollout: controls are only shown to users who are owners/admins of the target org publisher and members of the `openclaw` ClawHub publisher.
- Enforce version-level scan gates for explicit historical skill artifact access so a superseded malicious or pending ClawScan/VT version cannot be downloaded or served just because the current latest version is active.
- Document the source-management invariants, version security boundary, GitHub App operational setup, and internal UI rollout gate in `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md`.

## GitHub App setup notes

Required env:

- `GITHUB_APP_ID`
- `GITHUB_APP_SLUG`
- `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`
- `GITHUB_APP_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- optional `GITHUB_APP_STATE_SECRET`

App permissions/events:

- Contents: read-only
- Metadata: read-only
- Webhook URL: `/api/webhooks/github-app`
- Events: `push`, `installation`, `installation_repositories`, `repository`

Repository links are disabled by default after installation until an org admin enables sync. Org install completion requires a signed GitHub installation webhook claim matching the current user's GitHub account; if the redirect beats the webhook, retry completion after the webhook arrives.

During internal rollout, the UI only exposes GitHub Sync controls when the viewer is both an owner/admin of the viewed org publisher and a member of the `openclaw` ClawHub publisher. This is a UI/discoverability gate; backend GitHub sync functions still enforce the target publisher admin boundary.

## Security boundary note

GitHub sync publishes through the normal skill publish pipeline and queues the normal scanners for each new `skillVersions` row. Scanner completion only promotes verdicts to the skill-level moderation row when the scanned version is still latest, which prevents stale async results from overwriting the current latest verdict.

Because historical versions remain addressable by explicit `version` or `tag`, artifact-serving endpoints now also enforce the requested version's own ClawScan/VT fields. Download, card, raw file, and version-detail endpoints block malicious or explicitly pending historical versions; static-scan-only findings remain advisory when ClawScan/VT do not block the version; scan and verification endpoints remain readable so callers can inspect the blocked version's security context.

## Maintainer acceptance callouts

This PR introduces source-managed behavior only for skills that an org explicitly opts into GitHub sync:

- Existing manual org skills remain manual unless they get an active source link for a linked repository/path.
- Once a skill has an active source link, manual publishes to that source-managed skill are rejected unless the publish comes from the matching source link and repository/path provenance.
- To return a source-managed skill to manual publishing, an org admin must disable/unlink the source link first; disabled source links are no longer authoritative.
- Repository removal, installation deletion, and installation suspension disable affected repository/source links without touching unrelated manual org skills.
- Repository links are disabled by default after app installation; an org admin must explicitly enable each repo before sync can publish.

Maintainers should explicitly accept these source-management, unlink, and rollback semantics before merge. The remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live proof from a real configured GitHub App install/webhook/sync path.

## Review feedback addressed

- Vercel inline feedback: bounded `fetchGitHubInstallationRepositories` pagination at 100 pages / 10,000 repositories and fail closed if GitHub keeps returning full pages.
- ClawSweeper code feedback: restored shared `SkillCard` title/summary-before-badges ordering and added a regression test for that order.
- Internal rollout feedback: added a UI-only OpenClaw membership gate around the GitHub Sync panel and callback completion effect, with regression coverage for allowed and hidden cases.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: added a tracked runtime proof checklist to `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` for the real GitHub App validation pass once app credentials exist; local `.env.local` does not currently contain the required GitHub App credentials to honestly capture live install/webhook proof from this worktree.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: automated proof is below. Full real install/webhook runtime proof still requires creating/configuring the actual GitHub App and secrets; this PR is ready for that app-creation validation pass.

## Proof / validation

Latest pushed SHA: `ada7cf22`.

Current-head CI on `ada7cf22` was observed passing for `static`, `unit`, `types-build`, `packages`, `e2e-http`, `playwright-smoke`, `playwright-local-auth`, CodeQL, Vercel preview/agent review, repository dispatch, auto-response, and secret scanning. Older cancelled `auto-response`/`dispatch` entries are superseded runs from prior heads; the latest current-head entries are green.

Local commands run:

- `bunx vitest run src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx --reporter=dot` — 1 file / 5 tests passed.
- `bun run format:check -- 'src/routes/user/$handle.tsx' src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` — passed.
- `bun run lint` — passed.
- `bunx vitest run src/components/SkillCard.test.tsx src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 3 files / 11 tests passed.
- `bunx vitest run convex/downloads.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 2 files / 258 tests passed.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` — passed.
- `bun run lint:oxlint -- ./convex/githubApp.ts ./src/components/SkillCard.tsx ./src/components/SkillCard.test.tsx` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `bun run format:check -- specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` — passed.
- `bunx vitest run convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 1 file / 7 tests passed.
- `bun run format:check -- convex/lib/githubAppSync.ts convex/lib/githubCommon.ts convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts` — passed.
- `bun run lint:oxlint -- ./convex/lib/githubAppSync.ts ./convex/lib/githubCommon.ts ./convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts` — passed.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` — passed.
